pr.title: Fix itbox issues, mark as matching
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Fixed remaining itbox matching issues and promoted src/melee/it/items/itbox.c from NonMatching to Matching. The patch added/placed ItemStateTable it_803F5850, moved itBox_Logic1_Spawned/Destroyed below it_80286088, corrected spawned_gobj cleanup from efLib_DestroyAll to grKongo_801D8058, and adjusted MAX_ROT_VEL to the exact matching literal. decomp.dev reported a new 100% .data match for main/melee/it/items/itbox and a small .sdata2 improvement.

key_files.role: Main itbox implementation; changed includes, added ItemStateTable it_803F5850, corrected destruction helper, and adjusted MAX_ROT_VEL.

what_changed: Marked melee/it/items/itbox.c as Matching in configure.py.
Replaced the ef/eflib.h include with gr/grkongo.h in itbox.c.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F5850 near the top of itbox.c with nine rows of itBox_UnkMotionN Anim/Phys/Coll callbacks.
Moved itBox_Logic1_Spawned and itBox_Logic1_Destroyed to appear after it_80286088 in source order.
Changed itBox_Logic1_Destroyed so non-NULL xDD4_itemVar.box.spawned_gobj is passed to grKongo_801D8058 instead of efLib_DestroyAll before clearing the pointer.
Automated report showed main/melee/it/items/itbox .data improved from 0.00% to 100.00% (+144 bytes) and .sdata2 from 94.12% to 98.04% (+4 bytes).
smart_moves: Matched the emitted data by representing it_803F5850 as an ItemStateTable of function pointers rather than leaving the table absent/implicit.
Used the grKongo-specific cleanup symbol indicated by the corrected include and call site, avoiding the previous generic efLib_DestroyAll dependency.
Tuned a single floating-point constant to the bit-sensitive literal needed for .sdata2 progress.
Kept unknown behavior names as address/UnkMotion symbols instead of inventing semantics while still allowing the object to match.
Updated the build configuration only for the targeted object, making the matching-status change easy to audit.
decomp_lessons: For item files, ItemStateTable placement and function-pointer contents can be the deciding factor for .data matching.
Data and text order both matter: moving logic callbacks relative to spawn code and placing the state table early were part of the successful match strategy.
Subsystem-specific cleanup calls can affect matching; a plausible generic destroy helper is not necessarily the original function.
Floating-point literals should be chosen for exact binary output, not just mathematical equivalence.
When an item object becomes matching, remember to update configure.py from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
decomp.dev bot reports are useful evidence for deciding whether a PR truly improved matching; here the strongest evidence is automated, not human review text.
naming_conventions: Item lifecycle callbacks use itBox_Logic1_Spawned and itBox_Logic1_Destroyed.
State callbacks follow the pattern itBox_UnkMotionN_Anim, itBox_UnkMotionN_Phys, and itBox_UnkMotionN_Coll.
Unresolved or address-derived symbols were retained as it_803F5850, it_80286088, and it_8028655C.
Box-specific item state lives under xDD4_itemVar.box, including opened, spawned_gobj, rot_vel_x, and rot_vel_y.
configure.py matching status uses Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itbox.c") inside MatchingFor.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Declare the state table explicitly as ItemStateTable it_803F5850[] with exact state IDs and callback pointers, including NULL where the collision callback is absent.
Use precise constants for sdata2: MAX_ROT_VEL needed 0.05235987715423107 rather than the nearby 0.05235987901687622.
Replace a mismatching call target even when behavior seems similar: efLib_DestroyAll was replaced by grKongo_801D8058.
Let the required include follow the true call target: ef/eflib.h was dropped and gr/grkongo.h was added.
Validate data progress separately from code progress; the bot reported both a new .data match and an .sdata2 improvement.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The PR body was empty, so intent is inferred from the title, diff, and automated report.
decomp-dev[bot] reported for GALE01: linked code 30.83% (+0.13%, +5072 bytes), matched data 20.96% (+0.01%, +144 bytes), and linked data 28.84% (+0.02%, +264 bytes).
decomp-dev[bot] listed one new match: main/melee/it/items/itbox .data +144 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%.
decomp-dev[bot] listed one improvement in an unmatched item: main/melee/it/items/itbox .sdata2 +4 bytes, 94.12% to 98.04%.
searchable_terms: Fix itbox issues mark as matching
follow_up_queries: Search other item destructor callbacks for efLib_DestroyAll calls that may actually need subsystem-specific cleanup helpers.
Audit remaining itbox .sdata2 mismatch after the reported 98.04% result.
Look for other item files with missing or misplaced ItemStateTable data blocking .data matches.
When semantics are clearer, consider whether it_803F5850 and itBox_UnkMotionN names can be promoted beyond address/unknown names.
